content:
🌟 HeroicGamesLauncher | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a unified and open-source game launcher for users to access and play games from various digital distribution platforms on their desktop operating systems.
✨ Key Features: • Supports Epic Games Store, GOG, and Amazon Games. • Cross-platform compatibility (Linux, Windows, macOS). • Built with web technologies (TypeScript, React, Electron).
📖 Summary: Heroic Games Launcher is an open-source game launcher that allows users to manage and play games from multiple digital storefronts including Epic Games Store, GOG, and Amazon Games. It supports Linux, Windows, and macOS, leveraging web technologies for its development.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 folio | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: Generating sophisticated PDF documents programmatically within Go applications, including converting HTML content to PDF, creating reports, invoices, and documents with advanced features like forms, signatures, and barcodes.
✨ Key Features: • Layout engine for programmatic PDF creation
📖 Summary: Folio is a modern PDF generation library for Go, offering a comprehensive suite of features including a layout engine, HTML to PDF conversion, form creation, digital signatures, barcode generation, and PDF/A compliance. It aims to provide a fast and efficient way to create complex PDF documents directly within Go applications without external dependencies like browsers.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 planning-with-files | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a structured and persistent planning system for AI agents and developers using markdown files, mimicking the successful workflow of Manus.
✨ Key Features: • Claude Code skill for persistent markdown planning • Manus-style workflow pattern • Project and task management via markdown files • Session recovery and version management • Support for various IDE hooks and agent integrations
📖 Summary: This Python repository implements a Claude Code skill that enables persistent markdown planning, inspired by the Manus workflow pattern. It allows users to manage projects and tasks through structured markdown files, facilitating a more organized and efficient development process.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 maptoposter | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Creating personalized, aesthetically pleasing map posters of any city in the world using code.
✨ Key Features: • Generate city map posters • Customizable themes and styles • Uses OpenStreetMap data via osmnx • Exportable map designs • Multilingual support with custom fonts
📖 Summary: MapToPoster is a Python script that generates minimalist map posters of cities. It leverages OpenStreetMap data and Matplotlib to create customizable visual designs based on various themes and user-defined parameters.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
